testcase.title: Basic INI parsing and retrieval
testcase.description: Tests parsing of multiple INI sections with various formatting styles, comments, spacing, and internationalized keys. Also tests option removal and error handling.

test-step.test-step-column: Load a configuration string containing multiple sections and options.
Configuration is successfully loaded into a parser object.
Retrieve and sort the list of sections.
Section names match the expected list after sorting.
Retrieve option values from multiple sections with various formatting.
All option values are correctly retrieved and match expected results.
Remove an existing option and check that it no longer exists.
Existing option is successfully removed.
Attempt to remove a non-existent option and expect a False return.
Second removal attempt returns False, indicating option doesn't exist.
Attempt to remove an option from a non-existent section and expect an exception.
Removing from a non-existent section raises NoSectionError.

testcase.title: Test case sensitivity of section and option names
testcase.description: Verifies that section names are case-sensitive, while option names are case-insensitive.

test-step.test-step-column: Add two sections that differ only in case ("A" and "a").
Both sections are added and retained as separate entities.
Set options using mixed-case keys.
All mixed-case options are accepted without error.
Retrieve the option using a different case than used during setting.
Options are retrieved correctly regardless of case.
Remove an option using a case-variant key.
Option is removed successfully using case-insensitive key.
Parse a multiline option value and retrieve it.
The multiline value is parsed and retrieved correctly.
Load config with a default and verify it appears as a fallback.
Option fallback from DEFAULT section works as expected.

testcase.title: Test case-insensitivity in DEFAULT section
testcase.description: Ensures DEFAULT section option names are accessible in a case-insensitive manner.

test-step.test-step-column: Initialize config with default options using lowercase keys.
DEFAULT section is created with lowercase options.
Retrieve values using uppercase key.
Retrieval using uppercase key returns expected value.
Initialize config with capitalized keys.
DEFAULT section is created with capitalized options.
Retrieve values using exact key.
Retrieval using same case returns expected value.

testcase.title: Invalid INI parsing raises correct exceptions
testcase.description: Verifies that malformed INI input raises appropriate exceptions during parsing.

test-step.test-step-column: Attempt to parse a line with extra whitespace before the option.
ParsingError is raised due to bad spacing.
Attempt to parse a line with missing key but present value.
ParsingError is raised due to malformed key-value syntax.
Attempt to parse a line with missing section header.
MissingSectionHeaderError is raised due to absent section header.

testcase.title: Exception handling for missing sections and options
testcase.description: Verifies correct exception types are raised when accessing or modifying missing sections/options.

test-step.test-step-column: Call .sections() and .has_section() on a new config object.
Empty list is returned; has_section() returns False.
Call .options() on a non-existent section.
NoSectionError is raised.
Attempt to .set() an option on a non-existent section.
NoSectionError is raised.
Attempt to .get() an option from a non-existent section.
NoSectionError is raised.
Add a section and attempt to .get() a non-existent option.
NoOptionError is raised.

testcase.title: Boolean value parsing
testcase.description: Verifies that common truthy and falsy values are parsed correctly by

test-step.test-step-column: Create a config section with values like '1', 'TRUE', 'yes', etc.
All truthy values are parsed as True.
Create another section with values like '0', 'False', 'nO', etc.
All falsy values are parsed as False.
Add invalid boolean values (e.g., "foo", "2").
Invalid values are present in the config.
Use getboolean() to retrieve and assert truthy/falsy values.
Calls to getboolean() return correct boolean results.
Use getboolean() on invalid values and expect exceptions.
Calls to getboolean() on invalid values raise ValueError.

testcase.title: Duplicate section creation raises exception
testcase.description: Ensures that adding a section that already exists raises DuplicateSectionError.

test-step.test-step-column: Create a new configuration object.
Configuration is initialized successfully.
Add a section named "Foo".
Section "Foo" is added without error.
Attempt to add the same section "Foo" again.

DuplicateSectionError is raised when trying to re-add

the section.

testcase.title: Config write operation preserves formatting
testcase.description: Verifies that configuration written to a buffer matches the input formatting exactly.

test-step.test-step-column: Load a multiline configuration string.
Configuration is parsed without error.
Use the .write() method to write to a StringIO buffer.
Data is written to the buffer correctly.
Compare the output string with the expected formatted result.

Output matches the expected string exactly, preserving line

breaks and spacing.

testcase.title: Setting options using string subclasses
testcase.description: Ensures that setting option values using subclasses of

test-step.test-step-column: Create a configuration with an existing section.
Section is created successfully.
Set an option using a plain string.
Plain string is accepted and set correctly.
Set the same option using a custom string subclass.
Subclass of string is accepted without error.
Add a new option using both plain string and subclass.
New option is added successfully with both value types.

testcase.title: Read method returns list of successfully read files
testcase.description: Verifies that the

test-step.test-step-column: Attempt to read from a valid file and a non-existent file.
Only the valid file is returned in the list; the other is skipped.
Read from a valid file only.
Valid file is returned and parsed correctly.
Read from a non-existent file only.
Empty list is returned due to file not existing.
Read from an empty list of files.
Empty list is returned since no files were passed.

testcase.title: Interpolation resolves nested and recursive values
testcase.description: Verifies that string interpolation works correctly for nested references and that exceeding the maximum depth raises the appropriate error.

test-step.test-step-column: Load a configuration with multiple levels of interpolation (1, 9, 10, 11 steps).
Configuration is parsed successfully.
Retrieve an interpolated value with 1 level of depth.
Interpolation is resolved for 1-level reference.
Retrieve values with 9 and 10 levels of depth.
Interpolation works for deeper nesting up to the limit.
Attempt to retrieve a value with 11 levels to trigger InterpolationDepthError.
InterpolationDepthError is raised when exceeding the limit.

testcase.title: Interpolation fails with undefined reference
testcase.description: Ensures that referencing an undefined variable in interpolation raises InterpolationError with the correct metadata.

test-step.test-step-column: Load configuration with an option that references an undefined key.
Configuration loads successfully with missing reference.
Attempt to retrieve the value of the option.
InterpolationError is raised, containing section, option, and reference name.

testcase.title: Test resolution of interpolated items with defaults
testcase.description: Verifies that calling

test-step.test-step-column: Load configuration with default values and references using %(name)s and %(__name__)s.
Configuration is parsed without error.
Call .items() on the target section.
Items are retrieved correctly.
Sort the returned key-value pairs and verify correctness.
Interpolated values are resolved and included in the output.

testcase.title: Setting non-string types raises TypeError unless raw is used
testcase.description: Ensures that non-string types (int, list, dict) can be stored in raw mode, but raise TypeError when accessed with interpolation.

test-step.test-step-column: Create a section and set options with int, list, and dict values.
Non-string types are accepted and stored.
Retrieve each value using raw=True.
Raw retrieval returns the original type.
Attempt to retrieve each value without raw (interpolation enabled).
Standard retrieval raises TypeError for incompatible types.

testcase.title: RawConfigParser preserves interpolation placeholders
testcase.description: Ensures that when using RawConfigParser, interpolation references like %(foo)s are not resolved and are returned as-is.

test-step.test-step-column: Load a configuration with multiple levels of interpolation.
Configuration loads successfully.
Retrieve the value of each option that contains a placeholder.
Values are returned with interpolation placeholders untouched.

testcase.title: RawConfigParser returns unresolved values in items
testcase.description: Verifies that

test-step.test-step-column: Load configuration with default values and interpolation references.
Configuration is parsed successfully.
Call .items() on the target section.
Items are returned without resolving any interpolation.
Sort the result and verify that values include interpolation syntax.
Output matches expected literal strings with placeholders.

testcase.title: RawConfigParser allows non-string types
testcase.description: Ensures that RawConfigParser can accept and return non-string types without raising TypeError.

test-step.test-step-column: Create a new section in the config.
Section is added without issues.
Set options with int, list, and dict values.
Non-string values are stored successfully.
Retrieve each value using standard .get().
Retrieval returns values of original type with no error.

testcase.title: SafeConfigParser resolves safe interpolation syntax
testcase.description: Verifies that SafeConfigParser can correctly handle interpolation involving percent signs and preserves literal percent sequences.

test-step.test-step-column:
Load configuration with interpolated values that include

both placeholders and escaped percent signs.
Configuration is parsed successfully.
Retrieve an interpolated value using %(option1)s.
Placeholder interpolation is resolved.
Retrieve a value with a literal percent using %%s.
Escaped percent sequence is preserved in the final output.

testcase.title: Malformed interpolation strings raise ValueError
testcase.description: Ensures that setting options with malformed interpolation syntax (e.g. single % without a key) raises a ValueError.

test-step.test-step-column: Create a section and set an initial valid string.
Initial string is set successfully.
Attempt to set a string with an unmatched % token (e.g. '%foo').
ValueError is raised when using '%foo'.
Attempt to set another with trailing % (e.g. 'foo%').
ValueError is raised when using 'foo%'.
Attempt to set a mid-string malformed token (e.g. 'f%oo').
ValueError is raised when using 'f%oo'.

testcase.title: SafeConfigParser disallows non-string values
testcase.description: Ensures that SafeConfigParser enforces string-only values and raises TypeError otherwise.

test-step.test-step-column: Create a config section.
Section is created successfully.
Attempt to set int, float, and object as option values.
TypeError is raised for int, float, and object assignments.
Attempt to set a new option with each non-string type.
New options are not added if value is non-string.

testcase.title: Adding section named 'default' is invalid
testcase.description: Verifies that attempting to add a section named "default" (lowercase) raises a ValueError.

test-step.test-step-column: Create a SafeConfigParser instance.
Parser is created successfully.
Attempt to add a section named 'default'.
ValueError is raised due to reserved section name.

testcase.title: Adding section named 'DEFAULT' is invalid
testcase.description: Verifies that adding the reserved 'DEFAULT' section explicitly raises a ValueError.

test-step.test-step-column: Create a SafeConfigParser instance.
Parser is initialized successfully.
Attempt to add a section named 'DEFAULT'.
ValueError is raised when using the reserved name.

testcase.title: Sorted dictionary preserves section and option order
testcase.description: Ensures that using a sorted dictionary for storage preserves the ordering of sections and keys when the config is written to a string.

test-step.test-step-column: Create a configuration with multiple sections and unordered keys.
Configuration is stored with unordered insertion.
Write the configuration to a string.
Output is written successfully.
Compare the output against expected sorted section and option order.
Output shows sections and options sorted alphabetically.

testcase.title: Fuzz test for INIConfig parser robustness
testcase.description: Performs fuzz testing by generating randomized INI-formatted input containing a mix of comments, sections, options, and multiline values. Verifies that parsing and serialization do not raise exceptions and preserve config integrity.

test-step.test-step-column: Generate random key-value pairs, section headers, and comments using random tokens.
INI string is generated successfully with randomized components.
Construct a large synthetic INI string by combining the elements in random order.
A large synthetic INI string is constructed
Parse the synthetic string using INIConfig.
Parser handles all elements without raising exceptions.
Convert the resulting config back to a string.
Output string is generated without error.
Verify that the output can be parsed again without error.
Re-parsing the output string succeeds, indicating parser stability.

testcase.title: SectionLine rejects invalid lines
testcase.description: Verifies that lines which are not valid section headers return None when parsed by SectionLine.

test-step.test-step-column: Define a list of malformed or non-section strings.
Each invalid line returns None when parsed.
Attempt to parse each string using SectionLine.parse().
No exception is raised during parsing

testcase.title: SectionLine parses valid lines with comments
testcase.description: Verifies that SectionLine correctly extracts the section name and optional comment details.

test-step.test-step-column:
Create a list of valid comment lines starting with

'#', ';', or 'Rem'.
All input lines are valid comment lines.
Parse each comment line using CommentLine.parse().
Parsing returns a valid CommentLine object for each line.
Convert each parsed line to a string using str().
str() returns the original unmodified comment line.
Convert each parsed line using to_string().
to_string() returns the line stripped of trailing whitespace.

testcase.title: SectionLine preserves formatting on output
testcase.description: Ensures that calling

test-step.test-step-column: Parse a list of valid section lines.
str() returns the exact original line.
Call str() and to_string() on the result.
to_string() returns the line with trailing spaces removed.

testcase.title: SectionLine preserves spacing when name changes
testcase.description: Verifies that changing a section name maintains the original alignment and indentation of the line.

test-step.test-step-column: Parse a section line with a specific alignment and comment.
Section line with a specific alignment and comment is parsed
Change the section name to something longer or shorter.
Name changes are applied correctly.
Convert the object back to a string.
Output preserves spacing and comment alignment.

testcase.title: OptionLine parses valid lines and extracts components correctly
testcase.description: Verifies that OptionLine correctly parses option name, separator, value and optional comment fields from a variety of valid formats.

test-step.test-step-column:
Define multiple valid option lines using different separators and

comment placements.
Each line is accepted as valid input.
Parse each line using OptionLine.parse().
Parsing returns a valid OptionLine object.
Extract the name, separator, and value from each parsed line.
The name, separator, and value match expected values.
Extract optional comment separator, comment text, and comment offset.

Comment-related fields are either correctly populated or None,

depending on input.

testcase.title: OptionLine rejects invalid or misformatted lines
testcase.description: Ensures that lines which do not match valid option syntax are rejected and return None when parsed.

test-step.test-step-column:
Define lines that resemble options but are either misaligned,

incomplete, or structurally invalid.
Each invalid line is correctly identified as malformed.
Attempt to parse each line using OptionLine.parse().
Parsing returns None for each of them.

testcase.title: OptionLine preserves formatting during string conversion
testcase.description: Verifies that the string representation of OptionLine objects matches the original input formatting and spacing.

test-step.test-step-column:
1. Define a list of option lines with varying formats (spacing,

    separators, and comments).

  2. Parse each line using OptionLine.parse().

  3. Call str() on the parsed object and compare with the original line.

  4. Call to_string() and compare it with the stripped version

    of the original line.

1. All lines are parsed successfully.

  2. str() returns the exact original input including spacing.

  3. to_string() returns the same line but without trailing whitespace.

testcase.title: OptionLine preserves spacing and alignment when modified
testcase.description: Ensures that after modifying the option name and value, the output string retains the original alignment and spacing of the comment.

test-step.test-step-column: Parse an option line that contains an aligned comment.
The line is parsed successfully and the comment position is captured.
Change the option name and value.
New name and value are assigned without breaking structure.
Convert the modified OptionLine to a string.
str() returns a correctly formatted line with updated values.
Compare it to the expected output with correct alignment.
Alignment between value and comment remains consistent.

testcase.title: CommentLine rejects lines that are not valid comments
testcase.description: Verifies that lines which do not start with a valid comment prefix in column 0 are not parsed as CommentLine objects.

test-step.test-step-column:
Define a list of lines that resemble options, sections

or indented comments.

Each input line is valid for testing (e.g., starts with

[, contains =, or is indented).
Attempt to parse each line using CommentLine.parse().

Parsing returns None for each line, confirming they are not recognized

as valid comments.

testcase.title: CommentLine correctly parses and preserves formatting
testcase.description: Ensures that valid comment lines starting with

test-step.test-step-column: Define a list of valid comment lines with various prefixes and spacing.
Each line is recognized as a valid comment format.
Parse each line using CommentLine.parse().
Parsing returns a valid CommentLine object.
Convert the parsed object back to a string using str().
str() returns the exact original line including spacing.
Convert the parsed object using to_string().
to_string() returns the line without trailing whitespace.

testcase.title: EmptyLine identifies only blank or whitespace-only lines
testcase.description: Ensures that only lines which contain no visible characters (or just whitespace) are recognized as EmptyLine, and all other lines return None.

test-step.test-step-column: Create a list of lines that contain text, options, or section headers.
Lines with text are correctly identified as invalid for EmptyLine.
Parse each of those lines using EmptyLine.parse().
Parsing returns None for each non-empty line.
Create a list of lines that are completely empty or contain only whitespace.
Whitespace-only lines are accepted as valid EmptyLine instances.
Parse each of those lines using EmptyLine.parse().
Parsing returns a valid object for empty/whitespace lines.
Convert each valid EmptyLine to string using str().
String conversion of EmptyLine objects returns the original whitespace line.

testcase.title: ContinuationLine identifies and preserves indented lines
testcase.description: Verifies that lines with leading whitespace are recognized as continuation lines, and that their value and formatting are preserved correctly.

test-step.test-step-column:
Create a list of lines that are clearly not continuation lines

(e.g. section headers, options, comments).
Lines not intended as continuations are rejected.
Parse each of these using ContinuationLine.parse().
Parsing returns None for those lines.
Create a list of indented lines containing values.
Indented lines are accepted as continuation lines.
Parse each using ContinuationLine.parse() and access .value.
The .value returns the stripped value of the line.
Convert the parsed objects using to_string().

The to_string() returns the line without trailing whitespace

and with tabs replaced by spaces.

testcase.title: INIConfig parses repeated sections and preserves line numbers
testcase.description: Verifies that INIConfig correctly handles multiple repeated sections, resolves values based on last occurrence, and tracks the correct line numbers for options.

test-step.test-step-column: Load a configuration string with two '[section1]' blocks and one '[section2]'.
Config is parsed without error and recognizes both section1 and section2.

Use internal .find() method to retrieve specific options from

the parsed config.
.find() correctly locates each option.
Assert that each option contains the correct final value.
Final values reflect the last assigned value in repeated sections.
Assert that each option has the correct original line number.
Each option has the expected line number from the input string.
Create an iterator for all '[section1]' blocks using '.finditer()'.
Iterator over '[section1]' entries yields both blocks in correct order.
Iterate through values and validate correct content from both blocks.
Values from each block match expected keys and values.
Assert that the iterator raises StopIteration at the end.
StopIteration is correctly raised after the last section.

Attempt to access a missing section and option to ensure correct

exception handling.
Missing sections/options raise KeyError as expected.

testcase.title: INIConfig attribute-style access returns correct values
testcase.description: Verifies that values can be accessed using attribute-style access (

test-step.test-step-column: Load a config string with multiple sections and options.
Configuration is parsed correctly.
Access defined options using dot notation (e.g., p.section1.help).

All defined keys return their correct values using attribute-style

access.
Access a key with an apostrophe via getattr().
Special character keys (like "I'm") are accessible via getattr.
Access options that are not defined.
Accessing undefined options returns an Undefined instance.

testcase.title: New sections and options can be added using multiple access styles
testcase.description: Verifies that new sections and options can be added dynamically using various syntaxes including attribute-style, dictionary-style, and mixed forms.

test-step.test-step-column: Load the configuration string into an INIConfig object.
Configuration is parsed successfully.
Add a new section and key using dot notation (p.new1.created = 1).
Section and key are added using dot notation.
Add a new section and key using getattr() and setattr().
Section and key are added using dynamic attribute access.
Add a new section and key using dictionary-style access '(p.new3['created'] = 1)'.
Section and key are added using dictionary access.
Add a new section and key using bracket and attribute '(p['new4'].created = 1)'.
Mixed syntax access adds key successfully.
Add a new section and key using double-bracket dictionary access.
All key-value pairs are added correctly.
Retrieve all newly added values to confirm they were stored.
Each key returns the expected value when accessed.

testcase.title: INIConfig preserves the order of sections and options
testcase.description: Ensures that when iterating over sections and options, the original order in the config is maintained.

test-step.test-step-column: Load the configuration into an INIConfig object.
The config is parsed correctly.
Iterate over the top-level sections using list(p).
The section order is preserved (['section1', 'section2']).
Iterate over the options in section1 using list(p.section1).
The options in section1 appear in the correct order.
Iterate over the options in section2 using list(p.section2).
The options in section2 appear in the correct order.

testcase.title: Deleting sections and options updates the config structure and string output
testcase.description: Verifies that deleting options and sections removes them from the structure and updates the string representation accordingly, while preserving remaining content and formatting.

test-step.test-step-column: Load the config string into an INIConfig object.
Config is parsed without error.
Delete the option help from section1.
The option help is removed from the first section1 block.
Convert the config to a string and verify the output.

String output reflects the removal and retains formatting

and comments.
Delete the entire section section2.
Section2 is fully removed from the config.
Convert the config again and verify the final output.

Final output string reflects both changes while preserving

formatting of remaining content.

testcase.title: ConfigParser compatibility preserves section and option order
testcase.description: Verifies that

test-step.test-step-column: Initialize the parser with a default option using {'pi': '3.14153'}.
The parser is initialized correctly with default values.
Read the configuration string using .readfp().
Configuration is read without errors.
List all section names and assert their order.
Section order matches expected order from the config.
List all option names in section1 and assert their order including defaults.
All expected options are present and ordered as written.
List all items (key-value pairs) in section1 and assert the content and order.
Default values appear last and are included in the item list with correct values.

testcase.title: INIConfig handles invalid input lines by converting them to comments
testcase.description: Verifies that invalid INI syntax such as values outside sections or misplaced continuation lines are preserved as commented lines when

test-step.test-step-column:
Define two configuration strings with invalid syntax: A value outside

any section and Continuation lines not following any option.
Input strings are created and contain intentional syntax errors.
Load each configuration using INIConfig(StringIO(...), parse_exc=False).
INIConfig loads the input without raising exceptions due to parse_exc=False.
Convert each loaded configuration back to a string.
Output string conversion succeeds.

Compare the string output to expected versions where invalid lines

are commented out.
Invalid lines are preserved and transformed into comments in the final output.

testcase.title: INIConfig preserves and updates multi-line option values
testcase.description: Verifies that multi-line option values are preserved during parsing and can be updated programmatically while maintaining formatting.

test-step.test-step-column: Load a configuration string with a single option value spread over multiple lines.
Configuration is parsed successfully and contains a multi-line value.
Confirm that converting the config back to a string yields the same format.
The string representation of the config matches the original input.
Parse the multi-line value using .split.
The value is correctly split into a list of lines.
Modify the value by inserting a new line.
A new line is inserted into the correct position.
Update the config with the new value.
The modified value is saved back to the config without error.
Convert the config to a string and verify the updated format.
The output string reflects the updated multi-line value and preserves formatting.

testcase.title: INIConfig handles sparse multi-line values with empty lines
testcase.description: Verifies that an option value consisting of a multi-line string with blank lines is preserved correctly when parsed, modified, and serialized.

test-step.test-step-column:
Load a configuration string where an option's value

spans multiple lines.
Configuration is parsed and multi-line value is detected.

Confirm that converting the config back to a string matches

the original input.
Output string matches the original formatting.
Update the value with additional blank lines and content between them.
Updated value with blank lines is assigned successfully.
Add another option to the section.
Additional option is added without error.
Convert the updated config to a string.
Config is converted to string without formatting issues.
Verify that the final output preserves the new spacing and both options.

Output includes both the updated multi-line value and the

new option, with correct spacing.

testcase.title: Optionxform override in derived class respects case sensitivity
testcase.description: Verifies that subclassing ConfigParser and overriding optionxform preserves key casing and allows retrieval of similarly-named keys.

test-step.test-step-column: Create a subclass of ConfigParser with case-sensitive optionxform.
Parser preserves case-sensitive keys due to override.
Add section foo and set two options: bar and Bar with different values.
Keys bar and Bar are stored and retrieved independently.
Retrieve both keys and verify correct values are returned.
Values 'a' and 'b' are returned for bar and Bar respectively.

testcase.title: Direct assignment of optionxform preserves case-sensitive keys
testcase.description: Verifies that setting optionxform manually on a ConfigParser instance enables storing and retrieving multiple keys with differing cases.

test-step.test-step-column: Create a ConfigParser instance and assign str to optionxform.
ConfigParser accepts manual override of optionxform.
Add section foo and set keys bar and Bar with different values.
Keys bar and Bar are stored as distinct.
Retrieve both keys and check values.
Retrieved values match assigned ones.

testcase.title: Dynamically changing optionxform affects key resolution
testcase.description: Verifies that dynamically changing optionxform at runtime alters how keys are resolved, demonstrating case folding or preserving behavior.

test-step.test-step-column:
Create ConfigParser and set optionxform = str, then insert keys

with various cases.
All keys are stored successfully with original casing.
Change optionxform to str.upper and check lookup behavior.

Uppercase transformation causes lookup to resolve to latest

uppercase match.
Change to str.lower and repeat the lookup.
Lowercase transformation resolves to lowercase match.
Restore optionxform = str and verify final resolution.
Resetting to str resolves the key to original mixed-case match.

testcase.title: INIConfig supports file-like input with only readline()
testcase.description: Verifies that INIConfig can read configuration from an object that only implements the

test-step.test-step-column:
Create a fake file-like object implementing only readline()

with valid INI content.
The file-like object is initialized correctly.
Pass it to INIConfig._readfp() for parsing.
INIConfig parses the input without error.
Convert the resulting config back to string.
The output string is generated successfully.
Compare it with the original input.
The output exactly matches the original input.

testcase.title: ConfigParser supports file-like input with only readline()
testcase.description: Verifies that the ConfigParser-compatible interface accepts file-like objects implementing only

test-step.test-step-column:
Create a fake file-like object implementing only readline() with

valid INI content.
The file-like object is created correctly.
Pass it to ConfigParser.readfp() for parsing.
ConfigParser parses the input without error.
Write the parsed config to a new string buffer.
The output is written to buffer successfully.
Compare the output with the original input.
The output matches the original configuration input.

testcase.title: Multiline values span comments correctly on read
testcase.description: Verifies that

test-step.test-step-column:
Create an INI string where a value spans multiple lines and includes

an inline comment and blank line.
The config is loaded correctly without error.
Load the config into INIConfig using _readfp.
Multiline content is preserved across blank lines and comments.
Retrieve the multiline option value.
The value matches the expected multiline string.

testcase.title: Writing replaces multiline value with new single-line string
testcase.description: Verifies that assigning a new value to a previously multiline option replaces it with a single-line value in the output.

test-step.test-step-column: Load the multiline config into INIConfig.
The config is loaded correctly with the original multiline value.
Assign a single-line value to the multiline option.
The new value replaces the old one without error.
Convert the updated config to a string.
The output string contains the updated single-line option.

testcase.title: Reading an empty file results in empty config
testcase.description: Verifies that reading an empty file using INIConfig results in an empty configuration object.

test-step.test-step-column: Initialize INIConfig and load an empty string using _readfp.
The config is loaded successfully without errors.
Convert the config to a string.
The output string is empty, confirming no content exists.

testcase.title: Writing to empty config results in valid output
testcase.description: Verifies that assigning values to a previously empty config results in valid INI output.

test-step.test-step-column: Initialize INIConfig and load an empty string.
The config is initialized and remains empty after loading.
Add a section and an option with a value.
Section and option are added successfully.
Convert the config to a string.
Output string contains a valid section and key-value pair.

testcase.title: Custom dict type is not supported in RawConfigParser
testcase.description: Verifies that attempting to initialize RawConfigParser with an unsupported custom dictionary type raises a ValueError.

test-step.test-step-column: Attempt to create a RawConfigParser instance with dict_type='foo'.

A ValueError is raised, indicating that custom dictionary types are

not supported.

testcase.title: RawConfigParser preserves multiline values and defaults
testcase.description: Verifies that RawConfigParser correctly reads and writes multiline values, merges defaults, and removes empty lines from values.

test-step.test-step-column:
Initialize a RawConfigParser instance and load multiline

configuration string.
RawConfigParser loads the config without error.

Validate that options from DEFAULT section are merged into

other sections.
Merged defaults are visible in other sections.
Confirm correct parsing and preservation of multiline values.
Multiline values are parsed and cleaned correctly.
Serialize the config using write() and verify output format.
Output format is consistent and matches expected tidy layout.

testcase.title: ConfigParser handles multiline and default merging correctly
testcase.description: Verifies that ConfigParser reads a complex configuration with multiline values and default merging, and serializes it as expected.

test-step.test-step-column: Initialize ConfigParser and load config with DEFAULT and section values.
ConfigParser loads and merges values correctly.
Check merged keys and formatted output.
Multiline options are processed and stripped of extra spacing.
Serialize config and compare lines to expected output.
Output matches expected layout with correct indentation and order.

testcase.title: SafeConfigParser handles multiline values and default inheritance
testcase.description: Ensures that SafeConfigParser handles complex default merging and multi-line value formatting properly and writes a clean config.

test-step.test-step-column:
Initialize SafeConfigParser and load config string with

mixed formatting.
SafeConfigParser reads the input config without error.
Verify section and default resolution.
Merged values and options are resolved correctly.
Confirm output serialization matches cleaned structure.
Output string reflects normalized config layout.

testcase.title: RawConfigParser preserves multiline values and defaults
testcase.description: Verifies that RawConfigParser correctly reads and writes multiline values, merges defaults, and removes empty lines from values.

test-step.test-step-column:
Initialize a RawConfigParser instance and load multiline

configuration string.
RawConfigParser loads the config without error.

Validate that options from DEFAULT section are merged into

other sections.
Merged defaults are visible in other sections.
Confirm correct parsing and preservation of multiline values.
Multiline values are parsed and cleaned correctly.
Serialize the config using write() and verify output format.
Output format is consistent and matches expected tidy layout.

testcase.title: ConfigParser handles multiline and default merging correctly
testcase.description: Verifies that ConfigParser reads a complex configuration with multiline values and default merging, and serializes it as expected.

test-step.test-step-column: Initialize ConfigParser and load config with DEFAULT and section values.
ConfigParser loads and merges values correctly.
Check merged keys and formatted output.
Multiline options are processed and stripped of extra spacing.
Serialize config and compare lines to expected output.
Output matches expected layout with correct indentation and order.

testcase.title: SafeConfigParser handles multiline values and default inheritance
testcase.description: Ensures that SafeConfigParser handles complex default merging and multi-line value formatting properly and writes a clean config.

test-step.test-step-column:
Initialize SafeConfigParser and load config string with

mixed formatting.
SafeConfigParser reads the input config without error.
Verify section and default resolution.
Merged values and options are resolved correctly.
Confirm output serialization matches cleaned structure.
Output string reflects normalized config layout.

testcase.title: ConfigParser instances retain structure after pickling
testcase.description: Verifies that ConfigParser-compatible classes maintain structure and values after being serialized and deserialized via pickle.

test-step.test-step-column:
Initialize multiple ConfigParser-compatible objects and load

the test config.
Each config object is loaded correctly with expected data.
Serialize each object using pickle with all supported protocols.
Pickling and unpickling completes without error.
Deserialize the data and validate the structure and values.
Deserialized objects match the original in structure and content.

testcase.title: INIConfig retains structure after pickling
testcase.description: Verifies that INIConfig objects can be serialized and deserialized via pickle without losing data, formatting, or section layout.

test-step.test-step-column: Load a complex config into an INIConfig object.
INIConfig loads the input config and maintains section layout.
Serialize the object using pickle with all available protocols.
Pickling with each protocol succeeds.

Deserialize the result and check that structure and values

are preserved.
Deserialized objects retain all original content and formatting.

testcase.title: Changing comment syntax updates parsing regex
testcase.description: Verifies that the

test-step.test-step-column:
Set the default regex via change_comment_syntax(';#', True)

and verify result.
The default regex matches the original pattern.
Set Mercurial-compatible syntax using %;# and verify regex update.
Mercurial-safe regex is set correctly.

Call change_comment_syntax() with no arguments and confirm fallback

to Mercurial-safe regex.
Default call uses Mercurial-safe mode.

Use special characters in the syntax string and ensure they are

escaped properly.
Special characters are escaped and included in the regex.

testcase.title: Config with comment-style includes is parsed correctly
testcase.description: Verifies that comment lines using

test-step.test-step-column: Set Mercurial-style comment syntax using change_comment_syntax().
Comment style is updated to match Mercurial syntax.
Load a config containing % include using INIConfig.

Config is parsed correctly without ignoring or misinterpreting

comment lines.
Access a known value to ensure parsing succeeded.
The key username is accessible as expected.

Convert the config back to a string and confirm comment line is

preserved.
Output contains % include line unchanged.

testcase.title: INIConfig object can be sent between processes using multiprocessing.Queue
testcase.description: Verifies that an INIConfig object can be serialized, passed through a multiprocessing queue, and deserialized in another process while retaining access to nested values.

test-step.test-step-column: Create an empty INIConfig object and assign a nested value (cfg.x.y = '42').
INIConfig object is created and contains nested key-value structure.
Create two multiprocessing queues (q and w).
Multiprocessing queues are initialized successfully.
Put the INIConfig object into queue q.
Config is successfully placed into the first queue.
Start a new process which pulls the config from q, reads x.y, and puts it into queue w.
New process starts and retrieves the config object from the queue.
Wait for a result from queue w with a timeout.
The nested value is accessed without error and placed into the second queue.
Assert that the value returned is the original nested value.
The main process retrieves the correct value from queue w and matches it against expected result.

testcase.title: Tidy leaves empty INIConfig unchanged
testcase.description: Verifies that running tidy on an empty INIConfig does not introduce any content or whitespace.

test-step.test-step-column: Create an empty INIConfig object.
The config is initialized successfully and is empty.
Convert it to a string and verify it is empty.
Initial string output is an empty string.
Apply the tidy() function to the config.
tidy() runs without errors.
Convert the config to a string again.
Final string output remains an empty string.

testcase.title: Tidy appends newline to end of file if missing
testcase.description: Ensures that tidy adds a trailing newline to the end of a config file if one is not present.

test-step.test-step-column: Create a config with one section and one option.
Config is created and contains valid content.
Convert it to a string and verify it ends without a newline.
Initial output string does not end with a newline.
Apply tidy() to the config.
tidy() appends a newline during cleanup.
Verify that the final string output ends with a newline.
Final output ends with a single newline.

testcase.title: Tidy removes empty lines before the first section
testcase.description: Verifies that tidy removes leading blank lines from the beginning of the config.

test-step.test-step-column: Create a config string with blank lines before the first section.
Config string is successfully created
Load the config into an INIConfig object.
Config string with leading whitespace is loaded successfully.
Apply tidy() to clean the config.
tidy() removes the initial blank lines.

Convert the config to a string and verify that it starts with

the section header.
Final output starts directly with the section header.

testcase.title: Tidy removes unnecessary blank lines but preserves continuation lines
testcase.description: Verifies that tidy removes excessive blank lines between sections and options, while preserving properly indented continuation lines.

test-step.test-step-column: Create a config string with excessive blank lines and continuation lines.
Config is parsed with both extra blank lines and continuation lines.
Load the config into an INIConfig object.
tidy() removes blank lines that do not serve a formatting purpose.
Apply tidy() to clean the formatting.
Continuation lines are preserved without changes.
Convert the config to a string and compare to expected cleaned format.
Final output has a clean and compact structure without affecting data.

testcase.title: Tidy cleans config created via ConfigParser and preserves structure
testcase.description: Verifies that tidy can be used with a config object from

test-step.test-step-column: Define a multi-section INI string with excessive blank lines.
The config string is successfully defined for testing.
Parse the config using ConfigParser and .readfp().
ConfigParser loads the config and parses it correctly.
Apply the tidy() function to the ConfigParser object.
tidy() removes the excessive newlines without affecting data structure.
Convert the cleaned config to string using str(cfg.data).
The config is successfully converted back to a string.
Compare the output string with the expected tidy format.
The final output matches the expected tidy version in both structure and content.

testcase.title: ASCII input is parsed and serialized correctly
testcase.description: Verifies that standard ASCII INI strings are parsed successfully and that all values are retrievable and printable using

test-step.test-step-column:
1. Create an INI string containing only ASCII characters.

  2. Parse the string using INIConfig.

  3. Retrieve a known value from the config.

  4. Convert the config back to a string using str().

1. The INI string is parsed without errors.

  2. The expected value is retrieved correctly.

  3. Serialization works with str() and returns valid ASCII.

testcase.title: Unicode INI input without BOM is parsed correctly
testcase.description: Verifies that Unicode input without a Byte Order Mark (BOM) is parsed correctly and values with special characters are accessible, even if not ASCII-encodable.

test-step.test-step-column: Create a Unicode INI string with non-ASCII characters, excluding BOM.
Input string is parsed successfully.
Parse the string using INIConfig.
All values are retrievable.
Retrieve both values, including one with a non-ASCII byte ().
Non-ASCII characters are preserved.

Attempt to serialize the config with str().encode("ascii") and

expect failure.
Encoding to ASCII raises UnicodeEncodeError.

testcase.title: Unicode INI input with BOM is parsed correctly
testcase.description: Ensures that Unicode strings containing a BOM (

test-step.test-step-column:
Create a Unicode INI string that includes a BOM and non-ASCII

characters.
Input with BOM is parsed successfully.
Parse the string using INIConfig.
Both values are correctly accessible from the config.
Retrieve both options and verify values.
Unicode characters are preserved.

Attempt to serialize with str().encode("ascii") and expect

a UnicodeEncodeError.
ASCII encoding fails with UnicodeEncodeError.
